text: I have worked as a doctor for ten years.
Right now, I am pursuing a new license.
Hi, Ana Paula. Welcome. I am David, and I am a nurse here. I have worked in this hospital for eight years.
Nice to meet you, David. I am a doctor. My field is family medicine, and I studied in Brazil.
That is great. Before nursing, I actually worked as a paramedic, so I understand career transitions well.
Really? So you changed your field too. How long did that transition take you?
About two years. I went back to university and got my nursing degree. Now I specialize in emergency care.
That gives me hope. Right now, I am pursuing my license, and some days feel very demanding.
I am a doctor, and my field is family medicine.
I have worked as a physician for over ten years.
I studied medicine in Brazil and did my residency there.
Right now, I am pursuing my license here.
A career transition takes time, but experience stays.

Good morning, everyone, and welcome. Let me introduce myself. My name is Daniel Alves, and I am a doctor from Portugal. I studied medicine in Lisbon and I worked in a busy city hospital for almost ten years. Two years ago, I made a big transition and moved here to the United States. It has not been easy. I had to pass several exams to get my license, and I am still improving my English every day. Right now, I work as a resident, and I am learning how medicine works in a new system. My main field is family medicine, but I have recently started to specialize in care for older patients. My advice to all of you is simple: be patient with yourself. A career transition takes time, but your experience never disappears. Thank you, and I look forward to working with you.

Hi Ana Paula, it is Sofia! I just wanted to share some good news with you. Do you remember I told you I wanted to change my career? Well, I finally did it. For fifteen years I worked as a nurse, but I always dreamed of teaching. So last year I went back to university and got a degree in education. It was a demanding year, honestly, and there were moments when I wanted to give up. But I have just finished my first month as a health teacher, and I love it. My nursing expertise helps me every single day in the classroom. I know you are going through your own transition with the medical exams, so I really wanted to tell you: it is worth it. Keep going. You have worked so hard, and you will get there. Call me soon and tell me how you are doing. Bye!

First, say your name and your field, so people know who you are. Then give your main qualification and where you studied. After that, say how long you have worked and what you specialize in. Next, explain your current situation or the transition you are going through. Finally, say what you are working toward now and what comes next.
I have worked as a doctor for more than ten years.
My main field is family medicine.
I am going through a career transition.
I recently started to specialize in care for older patients.
It takes time to adapt to a new health system.
I have worked as a doctor for ten years.
I am pursuing a new license here.
I am a doctor, and my field is family medicine.
I have worked as a physician for over ten years.
I studied medicine in Brazil and did my residency there.
Right now, I am pursuing my license here.
A career transition takes time, but experience stays.
